zh.lifeSummary: 當Rebecca Clarke於1610年8月26日出生在英國諾福爾可班納姆時，她的父親Thomas Clarke Sr.43歲，母親Mary Canne35歲。她於1630年5月17日與Reverend Ralph Wheelock在英國諾福爾可福藍普林加姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1680年1月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可梅德費爾德享年69歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Rebecca
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from the Latin form of the Hebrew name Rebekah, borne by the wife of Isaac, who was the mother of Esau and Jacob (Genesis 24–27). The Hebrew root occurs in the Bible only in the vocabulary word marbek ‘cattle stall’, and its connection with the name is doubtful. In any case, Rebecca was Aramean, and the name probably has a source in Aramaic. It has always been common as a Jewish name; in England and elsewhere it began to be used also by Christians from the 14th century onwards and especially at the time of the Reformation, when Old Testament names became popular. It was very common among the Puritans in the 17th century, and has enjoyed a tremendous vogue in England since the latter part of the 20th century, among people of many different creeds. In Scotland this is found as an Anglicized form of Beathag .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Rebecca Clarke was born on 26 August 1610, in Banham, Norfolk, England, her father, Thomas Clarke Sr., was 43 and her mother, Mary Canne, was 35. She married Reverend Ralph Wheelock on 17 May 1630, in Wramplingham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 1 January 1680, in Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 69, and was buried in Suffolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rebecca Clarke est née le 26 août 1610, à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Clarke Sr., avait 43 ans et sa mère, Mary Canne, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Reverend Ralph Wheelock le 17 mai 1630, à Wramplingham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1680, à Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Suffolk, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rebecca Clarke am 26. August 1610 in Banham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Clarke Sr. 43 und ihre Mutter Mary Canne 35. Sie hat am 17. Mai 1630 in Wramplingham, Norfolk, England Reverend Ralph Wheelock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Januar 1680 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Suffolk, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rebecca Clarke è nata il il 26 agosto 1610 nel Banham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Thomas Clarke Sr., aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Mary Canne, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Reverend Ralph Wheelock il 17 maggio 1630, nel Wramplingham, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1680, nel Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Suffolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Rebecca Clarkeが1610年8月26日にイギリスノーフォルクバンハムで生まれた時，父Thomas Clarke Sr.は43歳，母Mary Canneは35歳であった。1630年5月17日にイギリスノーフォルクウランプリンガムでReverend Ralph Wheelockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1680年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクメドフィールドで69歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rebecca Clarke(이)가 1610년 8월 26일 영국 노르폴크 반함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Clarke Sr.(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Canne(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1630년 5월 17일 영국 노르폴크 램플링함에서 Reverend Ralph Wheelock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1680년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 메드필드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rebecca Clarke nasceu em 26 de agosto de 1610, em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Clarke Sr., tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Mary Canne, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Reverend Ralph Wheelock em 17 de maio de 1630, em Wramplingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1680, em Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Suffolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rebecca Clarke родилась 26 августа 1610 г. в местности банам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Clarke Sr. было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Canne, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reverend Ralph Wheelock 17 мая 1630 г. в местности рамплингхам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 1 января 1680 г. в местности медфиелд, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности суффолк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rebecca Clarke nació el 26 de agosto de 1610, en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Clarke Sr., tenía 43 años y su mamá, Mary Canne, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Reverend Ralph Wheelock el 17 de mayo de 1630, en Wramplingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1680, en Medfield, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Suffolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.
